title: Goniopteris eggersii
intro: Goniopteris eggersii is a species of fern native to Central and South America, characterized by its delicate fronds and distinctive leaf morphology. Traditionally, Goniopteris eggersii has been used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Goniopteris eggersii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Goniopteris eggersii is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is commonly used in traditional remedies to aid in the treatment of bronchitis, colds, and flu.
taxonomy: Goniopteris eggersii is classified within the plant kingdom as a species of fern, specifically belonging to the family Blechnaceae. This family is a part of the order Polypodiales and class Polypodiopsida. The class Polypodiopsida is further grouped under the division Pteridophyta, which is a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores. The phylum for this species is also known as Pteridophyta, further emphasizing its classification as a fern. At the kingdom level, it is classified as Plantae.
